checks.finding: design.md justifies its principal decisions: Technical Summary and Model Selection tie routing order to expected attempts and capability sufficiency; Architecture explains the dependency direction and avoidance of circular dependencies; Manifest Contract and Security Boundaries connect closed schemas, snapshots, hashes, and no-follow checks to isolation and integrity risks; Deployment / CI Plan explains why atomic release needs no feature flag.

design.md ## Open Questions explicitly states None and records the maintainer-approved routing, mappings, isolation, release, and delivery decisions; no unresolved question requires an owner, blocking declaration, or resolution path.

The four assumptions in design.md are bounded by approved specification evidence or explicit fail-closed behavior: Bash/PowerShell parity is required by REQ-010 and CI is specified in infra-spec.md; host capability reporting and failure behavior follow REQ-002; human reapproval authority follows REQ-004; and baseline 7df7318 is named by TEST-006. No empirically ungrounded set of non-trivial assumptions remains.

design.md ## Constraint Compliance addresses routing order, provider support, role floors, deterministic-script enforcement, terminal resume authority, context isolation, bounded fallback, runtime parity, and release synchronization. Manifest Contract, Isolation State Machine, Security Boundaries, Compatibility and Rollback, and the layer specifications further cover cost provenance, immutable snapshots, fail-closed validation, reviewer isolation, and rollback constraints without contradicting requirements.md.
No performance requirements found in requirements.md.

design.md ## Deployment / CI Plan identifies the target as an atomic repository/plugin release, requires seven GitHub Actions jobs, states that no environment variables, secrets, service deployment, or database migration are introduced, explains why no feature flag applies, and explicitly marks migration ordering not applicable.

design.md ## Data Plan explicitly states that no database or application-data migration is required and that historical reports remain readable. Release-file transition and reversal are separately planned through synchronized 1.5.0 publication and the pinned rollback inventory.

design.md introduces no network or third-party runtime integration. The internal host boundary is identified in Components, Model Selection, Backend Plan, and API / Contract Plan: its purpose is capability discovery and agent launch, its contract is the versioned capability registry and manifest schema, and unavailable qualifying models fail closed as model-tier-unavailable.

The design covers the requirements.md goal and REQ-001 through REQ-011 through routing, escalation, capability mapping, task/reviewer isolation, manifest integrity, reporting, parity, release, and rollback components. Frontend Plan and the layer specifications correctly exclude UI and service scope; no unsupported endpoint, data model, or user-facing feature is introduced.

design.md ## Test Strategy names concrete commands and evidence paths for the high-risk routing, authorization, context-isolation, manifest-integrity, parity, and rollback surfaces, including agent-model-routing, task-context-isolation, review-agent-isolation, rollback, run-all, and repository-validator suites mapped to TEST-001 through TEST-006.
